Scene.title: Prologue
Scene.description: Introduction of the STORYTELLERS and the ocean

Page.StageDirection: Darkness. A fog has settled across the floor as STORYTELLERS appear onstage one by one, in very neat black suits, ties. All of them should all feel connected — like multiple parts of a kaleidoscope.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STORYTELLERS
DialogueBlock.Line: I want you to consider for the moment the 71 percent of our planet that is cut off from us.
The part of our home that you may be able to see, maybe even step into. But the part of our home and mine that we can never fully know.
The ocean.
This is the ocean. This is the surface, where I'm standing right now.
If I take a step down into the deep with the ocean floor there...
The pressure builds. The water above pushes you down with gravity in a push and pull of nature.
Among humans, the smart and the scared will only dive so far before the ocean collapses their lungs and squeezes their bodies.About here.
The rest below us is largely unknown. There isn't much for us there but the darkness and the creatures that drift in the great shadows.
This is why you should fear the ocean. The things that go bump in the night are the easy explanations.
A trick of the mind.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STORYTELLERS
DialogueBlock.Line: The walls in your house cooling down, expanding and compressing with temperature fluctuations.
It's not the things that go bump in the night that you should fear.
It's the things that don't bump at all.
And on the floor of the ocean where creatures live in perpetual darkness, fighting for scraps that descend from the surface for an age, they only wait.
For it's only a matter of time before they decide to rise.
This is a story that starts with the void. A void that is very much staring back.
Also, we'll do the Cha Cha Slide.
Let's begin.

Scene.title: The Shipyard
Scene.description: Dockworkers drink recalled energy drinks; Squish rises

Page.StageDirection: They disappear into the middle of the night at a shipyard. A DOCKWORKER is alone, in high vis and unloading crates from a forklift. There's a sound from the void. He stops, looks around and keeps on working. A shadow approaches, and grabs him from behind, screaming.

DialogueBlock.Line: BAH, Scared ya bruh!
How do you come up with this stuff man? So good.
Dunno hey, it just comes to me. Like that. Just comes to me. I'm doing your handover. What have we got?
Energy drink recall. Gotta send them back. We got the order this afternoon.
Where's it from? The writing's all weird.
I dunno. Forms don't say much, I think they're from Russia. I can't tell. It all just looks like A's to me, Oi. You wanna try one?

Page.StageDirection: He pries open a corner of the lid and pulls out two cans of energy drink.

DialogueBlock.Line: I dunno bruh. If they're being recalled wouldn't they be not good to drink?
It won't kill you.

Man, you know I'm down for doing things that don't make sense. I wear tank tops and beanies at the same time. But what if it does kill us? Then we'd be dead. Plus, it's like foggier than it usually is. Like it's more foreboding and swirly. I'm getting bad vibes hey.
Ooh. I don't want bad vibes only.
Yeah, it's about good vibes only.
I want good vibes only.
Yeah. Good vibes. Yew yew.

Page.StageDirection: All DOCKWORKERS throw shakas.

DialogueBlock.Line: But man, we have to bust ourselves full-time for 72 thousand a year with comprehensive benefits. We should at least get something out of the deal. Sack up and get a drink up you.

Page.StageDirection: He tosses a can of the stuff.

DialogueBlock.Line: Fair point.
See?

Page.StageDirection: They take a long sip out of the cans. Suddenly, he starts choking. His friend starts panicking.

DialogueBlock.Line: Mate? You okay? Do I need to call someone? Oh no bruh. We're done. We are so done.

Page.StageDirection: He continues to choke, but the choking eventually turns to laughter.

DialogueBlock.Line: You massive lad. I knew this was a G up.
I know right? So good hey. Best G up.
I thought you were done.
Nah, my left arm feels a bit numb is all.
Oh.
All good that comes and goes all the time. Come on, let's get this done, I'll be in the doghouse if I'm late again tonight. Know what I mean? Hey? Hey?
Too right.
Not wrong.
Too right.
Not wrong.
Too right.
Not wrong.

Page.StageDirection: They all laugh. And sigh.

DialogueBlock.Line: I'm actually pretty unhappy hey.

Page.StageDirection: They both take another long sip and throw the cans over the edge of the dock. The cans sink to the bottom of the ocean, leaking energy drink as they go. The shadow of a small octopus swims into the light. He continues to swim up, rising, until he reaches the cans, He begins to shake violently, then comes to a stop. He swims now with much more purpose, towards the surface, and breaks the surface of the water. He bobs in the water, searching. In the distance, a sign glows, reading PET STORE. The octopus makes a small sound, eerily close to laughter.
A TITLE SEQUENCE of some sort.

Scene.title: The Quad
Scene.description: Stella, Tony, and Priya discuss holidays and toilet licking

Page.StageDirection: The middle of the school yard. The start of a new term, new year. STELLA is sitting in the yard with TONY. TONY is eating M&Ms.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: ... So then I went to stay with my Dad for the rest of the holidays, and there's like this huge BMX track out the back of his house, and these kids ride there every Saturday and I saw this big kid break his arm and the bone was sticking out and everything and he was and then we had KFC. So holidays were good I guess.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Ive never seen your dad before.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: He's not around here much. He lives closer up near the bay. Like past the movies. The cheap one, not the good one.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Is he cool?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: Uh yeah. He buys me KFC and then he leaves me on my own to do whatever for like the whole weekend. He's like the perfect Dad.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Did you wash your school uniform.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: You don't have to. They're like jeans.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Um, wow. Who told you that?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: Dad. He also said hed teach me how to do a sleeper hold for my birthday.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: On you?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: I dunno. He didn't say.
What did you do on the holidays?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: I played Overwatch mostly.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: I've already beaten it.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: You can't beat it Overwatch. That's not the point.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: Well I did. Yew.

Page.StageDirection: PRIYA runs in.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: You guys! Dakota said that Aaron Anderson just licked a seat in the boys' toilet for 2 dollars and it was rank and now his breath smells like toilet duck.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Priya, what? Why? Why are you telling us?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: It was awesome.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: Once I saw a guy lick a dead body for twenty cents.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: Your stories are such BS Tony. Leave them in Grade 9 with flossing. Nobody does it anymore. And you need to stop eating so much sugar.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: You're not my mum.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: I dont have to be to tell you what you eat is straight up killing you.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: Not even. I actually feel stronger. My cousin once ate a whole box of LCM's and he was able to slow down time.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Tony, your cousin was lying. You can't bend time by ealing sugar.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: You can. Jack said he was able to move so fast people couldn't even see him anymore.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: People saw him running around the Rebel Sport screaming without pants. My sister was working and she said they had to throw out the yoga mats.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: It's not healthy.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: Licking a toilet would be better for you.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: You know for the smartest Grade Tenner you're still pretty dumb, Priya. You can't argue with something my cousin said.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: I am the smartest Grade Tenner. I'm taking a uni class this year.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: Well I can jump and touch the top of doors. We all have stuff we're good at.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: I bet you'd lick the toilet seat for one dollar.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Priya, don't. You know he will, stop encouraging him.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: I'm not encouraging him, he's just following his own pattern of behaviour. Risk and reward. It's important for him to learn and reflect so he can change. And he'll lick pee so it's funny. Won't you?

Page.StageDirection: TONY thinks about it.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: Show me the dollar.

Page.StageDirection: PRIYA does. TONY and her shake hands.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Tony, you're in Grade 10 now. We've gone through a lot of growth over the holidays. Don't undo it.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: It's fine. Aaron licked it first, so it's clean. I know what I'm doing.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: I don't think it works like that.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: Takea selfie of you doing it. 'm not going in there. It smells from across the school. Why does every boy's toilet just smell so specific?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: This is going to be the best year ever!

Page.StageDirection: TONY runs off to lick the toilet. TYSON skates in.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TYSON
DialogueBlock.Line: Hey gronk. Mum texted me to say I had to give you lunch. You left before I could.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: She could've done it herself.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TYSON
DialogueBlock.Line: She left for work early. You weren't up.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Youre going to get a uniform warning again with those shoes.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TYSON
DialogueBlock.Line: Let them. I'm here, I got myself and I got you here. If they want to warn me that's fine. Their choice to be petty. You get your teachers yet?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Yes, thank you Tyson, you can go now. Not a baby.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TYSON
DialogueBlock.Line: Yeah alright Grade 10s. You know you're in the invisible year, right? You're like the middle aged child of the school. Stuff gets weird in Grade 10. Watch out.
Hey Priya.

Page.StageDirection: PRIYA shrinks.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: UhyeahheyTyson.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TYSON
DialogueBlock.Line: How's your sister?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: Yeahgood

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TYSON
DialogueBlock.Line: She mention me at all?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: NoIdunnoshe'sdumbwhatever.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TYSON
DialogueBlock.Line: I thought she enjoyed the movie. Tell her I said hey, okay?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: YeahalrightIwill.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TYSON
DialogueBlock.Line: Sweet. Stay gold, big kids.

Page.StageDirection: He skates away.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Did my brother and your sister go on a date?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: He came to my house. Where I live.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Oh my god.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: And my mum and dad weren't home and Sonam and him were on the couch and let's just say I'm glad I had my headphones in.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: UGGH I've got a mental image!

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: That gave me a mental image!

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Ewwwwwwww

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: Ewwwwwwww

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: We'd be related if they got married.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: We could do adult things together like have brunch and complain about tax. Hey.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: Let's have each other's back this year, okay? If Tyson's right we might just need to hang on a bit.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Yeah, for sure. I think we'll be fine. This is going to be a big year.

Page.StageDirection: KID [off] Oi Tonksie what're you doing? Tonksie!
Offstage, sounds of disgust and horror from near the toilet blocks. TONY appears.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: I did it. I filmed it and everything. I'm the winner.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: That's not what Id call you.

Page.StageDirection: She throws him the dollar. MISS DUANE, the Grade Ten Bio teacher, enters.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: Tony Tonks, get over here now.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: What? I do?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: Whenever I hear screaming in the quad I know you'll have something to do with it. Do I really have to tell you that licking a toilet isn't the kind of behaviour that we expect from you?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: But Priya said shed give me a dollar if] did it.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: I didn't think hed actually do it. I lost a dollar. I'm the victim!

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: Well, you can talk about it with each other in detention later today.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: Miss!

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: Miss!

Page.StageDirection: MISS DUANE pulls out her detention slips.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: On the first day, too. That's a red hot record. Let's see... Tony Tonks, Priya Pingali, and Stella Bunt.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: What? I didn't do anything!

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: You were here too. Why didn't you stop it?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: I don't know.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: And when these two were making a bet about putting a tongue on a toilet, where did you go?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Nowhere. I was here.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: So you saw your friends doing something wrong and you did nothing. What if Tony gets sick? How are you going to feel then knowing you could have stopped it? What if he dies? What if he gets blood poisoning and the last thing he ever hears is the sound of his own rattling chest as he tries to keep alive, not ever sure as to why he even wants to be alive and in so much agony? What'll you do then miss?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: Can that happen?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: I tried to tell him —

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: You better learn to assert yourself, miss, or people will walk all over you. One minute your friend is licking toilets, the next thing your partner is distant and you drive to school one morning with the handbrake on because you were too distracted trying to do your makeup in the mirror because you woke up late because you were up all night writing the erotic novel that'll be your ticket out of your here because you cannot deal with that bell and whatever song Janine in the front office likes this week.

Page.StageDirection: The school bell rings. It's a terrible song — something like Brave by Sara Bareilles.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: Great, there goes my break. Let's make it detention for the rest of the week.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: ALL
DialogueBlock.Line: NO!

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: This afternoon after school. I'll have some jobs for you. Now off to assembly. And no more licking things. We haven't had a quarantine since the peanut outbreak at the school musical and I'm not going back there again. Assembly. Now.

Page.StageDirection: MISS DUANE goes.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: Thanks a lot, you butt munch! You got me into trouble.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: She's right, Stella. You can be so irresponsible.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: I think you can look after yourself, Tony.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: It's not my fault ''m impulsive. I'm a spring baby. You really dropped the ball on this one.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: Dont blame her! Oh God. This is bad. This is really bad.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Look, it's not a big deal. We'll just go to detention and that'll be it. At least we're doing it together.

Page.StageDirection: MISS DUANE appears again as the other students scatter onstage to sit for assembly.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: Guys. Thank you. Move. Jordan! Stop running. Straight lines please.

Scene.title: Assembly
Scene.description: Mr. Fowler addresses the school; SRC discusses the Pre-formal

Page.StageDirection: The bell rings again. Whatever song it is, stick with it for the rest of the show. We're in Assembly. MR FOWLER is addressing the students.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: FOWLER
DialogueBlock.Line: — and as we welcome all of you back for another exciting year, a reminder that the demountables are out of bounds until the snake nests are dealt with. Do not approach the snakes. Also, I can't believe that I need to address this —

Page.StageDirection: One of the kids is talking.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: FOWLER
DialogueBlock.Line: I'll wait.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: FOWLER
DialogueBlock.Line: This is eating into your lunch time everyone.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: FOWLER
DialogueBlock.Line: I need to remind students not to make bets on licking toilets. Some of our Grade Tens who should know/ better

Page.StageDirection: A KID

DialogueBlock.Speaker: KID
DialogueBlock.Line: Tonksie!

DialogueBlock.Speaker: FOWLER
DialogueBlock.Line: Than to be licking toilets.

Page.StageDirection: Some laughter.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: FOWLER
DialogueBlock.Line: Oh you all think that's funny? Getting on your knees and treating a toilet like a lollipop? In a 30 degree tin shed thats filthy with old urine? Hot porcelain? That's funny? Well laugh it up.

Page.StageDirection: If the laughter continues:

DialogueBlock.Speaker: FOWLER
DialogueBlock.Line: I'll wait again. Your time, everyone. Not mine. Now I won't see it happen again. And now, I'd like to invite the SRC representatives, Madison, Maddison and Madyson who'll be giving a short reminder on the Year 10 pre-formal.

Page.StageDirection: The SRC take the podium. Scattered applause. The girls are very polished presenters.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: MADISON
DialogueBlock.Line: Welcome everyone to 2020. We made it. Wow. Whod have thought? For those of you who don't know me, my name is Madison, and It's my pleasure to be serving as your President of the SRC!

Page.StageDirection: She holds for an applause she'll never hear.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: MADISON
DialogueBlock.Line: Also, this is Maddison and Madyson with a Y.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: BOTH
DialogueBlock.Line: MADDISON

DialogueBlock.Speaker: MADDISON
DialogueBlock.Line: We've got a packed agenda coming up this year with moving toward plastic free packaging in the canteen and a second clothing donation drive this Wednesday!

DialogueBlock.Speaker: MADYSON
DialogueBlock.Line: Save the turtles guys!

DialogueBlock.Speaker: MADDISON
DialogueBlock.Line: [sotto] | need you to take a step back.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: MADYSON
DialogueBlock.Line: Sorry.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: MADDISON
DialogueBlock.Line: And we haven't even begun to talk about the event of the school calendar, the Year 10 Pre-formal! Here to tell you more is Madyson with a Y!

DialogueBlock.Speaker: MADYSON
DialogueBlock.Line: Thanks, Maddie.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: MADDISON
DialogueBlock.Line: Maddison.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: MADYSON
DialogueBlock.Line: Good morning students and teachers. At the Royal Wedding to her Prince Harry, Meghan Markle disrupted tradition, and chose to carve a stronger path for herself and upsetting a system by marrying into it. And we hope you'll embrace the same spirit of rebellion and make bold choices at the event of the school calendar ...

DialogueBlock.Speaker: MADISON
DialogueBlock.Line: Move over, HPV vaccines!

DialogueBlock.Speaker: ALL 3
DialogueBlock.Line: The Year 10 Pre-Formal!

DialogueBlock.Speaker: MADDISON
DialogueBlock.Line: With this year's theme, we decided to take things out of the quad and... Under The Sea.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: MADYSON
DialogueBlock.Line: But while the SRC will be in charge of planning, creative direction, hosting, most of the work, we really want you to feel empowered to make this your night. But there are some notes before we can let you start planning. In order to stay on the aesthetic the following colour dresses are banned: White. Off White.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: MADISON
DialogueBlock.Line: The colour, not the label. Haha.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: MADDISON
DialogueBlock.Line: Periwinkle. Cyan.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: FOWLER
DialogueBlock.Line: Okay, that's enough. Thank you girls.

Page.StageDirection: More scattered applause.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: FOWLER
DialogueBlock.Line: Alright, thank you everyone, now off to your next period please. Move responsibly.

Scene.title: Science Lab
Scene.description: Detention begins; Miss Duane introduces Squish the octopus

Page.StageDirection: Later in the day. School has ended, MISS DUANE is in the science lab with the kids.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: Now I know you might think detention bites the big one, but I want you to think of this as a time to have a think about what the rest of this year means for each of you. You can turn it around still. You have the chance to really make an impact on your school and make it the best it can be for everyone!

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: What are we doing, Miss?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: You're cleaning that fish tank. And once you've done that, you can help our new friend move in.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Excuse me?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: It's part of our studies on marine life. The grade tens last year voted to get a turtle, but everyone lost interest and the next thing you know, I have to poke it with a pencil so see if it was still alive. Not for a while.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: DUANE

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: That's horrible.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: Have you ever had a pet turtle? They eat and die, that's pretty much it. Not particularly exciting. Anyway, the curriculum has shifted a little again, so I thought we could try a new friend this year.

Page.StageDirection: She lifts up a big fishbowl. Inside is a tiny octopus.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Wow, a squid!

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: Close, Stella. This is an octopus. One of the smartest creatures in the ocean, and maybe even the planet.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: Ha! Nerd.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: Clean the new tank, and then maybe we can decide on a name. I'll be outside.

Page.StageDirection: She opens Tinder on her phone and leaves. PRIYA pulls out her Switch, and Tony starts eating a full packet of biscuits. STELLA starts tidying the tank.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: Its so weird looking. I always thought theyd be cuter.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: It probably thinks we look weird too.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: Can I pat him?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: It'll probably sting you. And it'll just stick to you and you won't be able to get rid of it.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: He can pet it.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: Do you think he knows what we're saying? Maybe he's hungry. Do they eat biscuits?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: Oh my God, no!

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: I'm just wondering. I have a curious mind. Don't crush my spirit. | want to see what he feels like.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Just leave him be, Tony. Hes probably still getting used to his environment. Plus, you don't know, he could be dangerous. Some of the smallest creatures of the ocean are the deadliest.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: You're so boring sometimes Stella. Don't you ever want to do anything fun?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: I do heaps of fun stuff.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: Like what?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Last night I went down this Youtube hole about the history of using/ bronze as ...

Page.StageDirection: She's met with groans.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: But that's not really fun though, is it? You have to live a little.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: Leave her alone, you ming. Not everyone is meant to be exciting all the time.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: But I'm exciting some of the time, right?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: Well ... you're stable. And reliable. That's important too.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: Yeah. Like, I'm like this biscuit. Priya and I are the chocolate outside. We're the bits that people remember and love because we taste good and were fun. And you're like the icing in the middle. You keep everything together, but nobody knows what you're actually made of.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: I dort want to be icing.

Page.StageDirection: She thinks for a minute for something to do, and then starts attempting to breakdance.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: It's okay, you don't have to do that. You're still our friends.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: Stop. It hurts.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Check it dudes!

Page.StageDirection: She attempts a floor routine, but bumps the fish tank, shaking it.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: Look out!

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: I didn't mean to!

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: Is he okay? Poor little guy.

Page.StageDirection: MISS DUANE returns, still on her phone.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: Are we done?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: I don't think so, Miss.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: Don't worry about it, the guy at the pet store said cleaning wasn't that big of a deal. I also got a bit of a vibe from him. So.

Page.StageDirection: The kids stare blankly.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: Let's think of a name for him and then we can go home.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: What about Tony?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: Sure.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: What about Oscar?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: Nice.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: 'Tony?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: Snickers?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: 'Tony?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: Glitter?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: I'm hearing a lot of support for Tony.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: He's really squishy looking. What about Squish?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: 'That's actually pretty good. Squish.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: That's not fair!

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: Okay, Squish it is. Sorry, Tony.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: It's okay, his attention span is pretty short.

Page.StageDirection: TONY is eating again.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: I'm wearing new socks today.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: Right then, home time. Let's go. Scoot. Out.

Page.StageDirection: They all leave, STELLA is lagging behind.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: That means you too, Stella.

Page.StageDirection: MISS DUANE goes, STELLA goes back to the fish tank.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: It's lovely to meet you, Squish. My name is Stella Bunt. Is it comfy in there? I guess it's pretty different from the ocean, but you'll get used to it. I hope we can get to know each other. I dont really know much about the ocean if I'm being honest. My brother and I watched a movie about a whale and we thought it was going to be funny, but it wasnt. So if anybody tells you to watch Blackfish, maybe say no.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE [off]
DialogueBlock.Line: Stella!

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: I'm sorry, listen to me go on. I have to go. You're a good listener, buddy.

Page.StageDirection: She turns to leave. Suddenly, a small pebble is thrown from the tank and hits her in the back of the head, She picks up the pebble and leans in close to the tank. SQUISH slams against the glass, trying to reach STELLA. TONY and PRIYA return and SQUISH goes back to behaving normally.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: Stella? Let's go already.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Uh ... you know what's weird?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: What?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: I think Squish threw something at me.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: The octopus threw something at you?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: That is stupid. Octopuses can't be that smart. It probably fell out of the roof. Remember when the beehive fell out of the roof in the music rooms?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: But we don't have music rooms.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: We don't now.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: There were a lot of bees.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: I'm just saying stuff like that isn't unusual.

Page.StageDirection: The classroom lights turn off, and the kids leave.

Scene.title: Stella's Home
Scene.description: Stella and Tyson watch a news report on the dockworkers

Page.StageDirection: STELLA is now at home with TYSON, both on their laptops. The TV is on in the background.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TYSON
DialogueBlock.Line: So ... Tony only asked for a dollar for licking the toilet?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Yeah. And then we all have detention for the rest of the week.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TYSON
DialogueBlock.Line: Why do you have detention though? You didn't lick the toilet. Did you?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: No!

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TYSON
DialogueBlock.Line: I'm proud of you.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Shut up. Miss Duane said that I should have stood up and said it was a bad idea.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TYSON
DialogueBlock.Line: I wouldn't take Miss Duane too seriously. She misspelled 'conduct' on my report card when she taught me. And “Tyson.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: I don't think it's fair though. I'm being punished because Tony is gross.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TYSON
DialogueBlock.Line: Correct. You are. You're learning that things are unfair, and life is unfair. You're really growing up today. So proud.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TV
DialogueBlock.Line: We now go live to Moon Beach, where two men reported missing yesterday have been found dead, two kilometres from where they were last seen at their place of work at the dockyards.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Oh no.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TV
DialogueBlock.Line: We regret to advise that we cannot show you any disturbing images at this time, as the bodies are reported to be severely disfigured. One bystander is quoted as saying 'they look mutated and stuff? 'I threw up in my mouth a little' and 'It's pretty awesome: We spoke to some of the men's colleagues earlier, before they too died suspiciously.

Page.StageDirection: Some of the DOCKWORKERS from before being interviewed.

DialogueBlock.Line: Just goes to show you never know when it's your time, hey?
One minute you're drinking energy drink that so called experts call toxic and the next ...
Death sounds.
But they died doing what they loved, you know?
Geeing each other up.

Page.StageDirection: They raise cans of the drinks to sip.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: REPORTER
DialogueBlock.Line: Aren't those the same drinks?
Oh no. Worst gee up.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TV
DialogueBlock.Line: To see the full, uncut footage, join us at 11 for our Sexy News Bulletin, or follow me on Twitter for all of the uncut action.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TYSON
DialogueBlock.Line: Ugh. Who watches the news on TV anymore?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Ty, I was watching that!

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TYSON
DialogueBlock.Line: Stick to podcasts, you little weirdo. News is for boomers. Get to bed.

Page.StageDirection: They leave. In the shadows, a silhouette of an octopus appears, crawling in the middle of a street. A can of energy drink lies at the side of the road. The octopus continues to crawl, until he touches some of the spilled liquid. Suddenly, he shakes and begins to grow larger and more muscular, until he is the size of a fully grown man. He raises his arms and roars.

Scene.title: Science Lab
Scene.description: Squish is missing; Greg (the "New Kid") is introduced
Scene.Page:
Science Lab PRIYA You know when you talk? Sometimes you just shouldn't. STELLA Um ... Guys? TONY Friends share stories, Priya! STELLA Guys? PRIYA I tell you about fun things, not fungal infections! STELLA Guys! PRIYA What? The kids enter the science lab. They have the cleaning equipment ready to work.
TONY ... And then the doctor said that I shouldn't pick at the warts because they'll just grow back. I had three on my knee, but now they're just one big one. I call it Diglett.
PRIYA You know when you talk? Sometimes you just shouldn't.
STELLA Um ... Guys?
TONY Friends share stories, Priya!
STELLA Guys?
PRIYA I tell you about fun things, not fungal infections!
STELLA Guys!
PRIYA What?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Where's Squish?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: In the fish tank.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: No he isn't.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: Did he get out? Can octopuses even do that?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: What if a cat ate him? A cat ate my bird once. I found its head under the bathmat.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: We're going to get in even more trouble, you guys! They're probably really expensive.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Okay, well —

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: Do something! I can't look at another head!

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Let's just look around the room. It was locked last night, there's no way he could have gotten out, so he must be here somewhere.

Page.StageDirection: MISS DUANE enters, on her phone.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: How's the cleaning coming along? Ready for afternoon tea?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: We got the sandwich out of the bubblers outside, and we're
jast about to start sweeping.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: And we're looking for Squish!

Page.StageDirection: Death stares from the girls.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: Sorry. Because we lost him.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA/PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: Come on!/Oh my god.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: It's alright, it happens. I'm not angry with you. Tony, thank you for telling the truth. So I'll tell you all the same thing my mother told me when I lost my dog. It's not on a farm, it's not in the wild having fun, it's dead. It's dead and it's not coming back. Okay?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: I'm sorry, Miss.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: It's okay, Stella. We'll find another pet, but for now I havea
special job for you.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Is it for detention?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: No, this one is an actual job. There's a new student in our
grade who'll be starting tomorrow, and he's a little shy.
I thought you might be the perfect student to get him
settled. Can you do that for me?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Sure.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: Excellent. Greg?

Page.StageDirection: GREG enters. He is the octopus, now the size of a man, but is not so cleverly disguised as a little boy in school uniform. On his head is a giant, hideous paper mache head painted with a smiley face, with a hole for his mouth, full of teeth that are coated with vile black drool.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: L know he looks a little different, but just make sure that he
feels welcome and you'll be friends before you know it!

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: Sure.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: Have some afternoon tea and I'll be back to check on you.

Page.StageDirection: They stare at each other, frozen.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: My afternoon tea is outside.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: I also have to go.

Page.StageDirection: They leave quickly.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: It's nice to meet you, Greg. I'm Stella Bunt. I know it must
be hard having a new school. It's pretty easy to get lost
here. Did anybody show you where anything is'

Page.StageDirection: Silence.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Okay. Do you know anybody from this school?

Page.StageDirection: Silence.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: I mean. It's okay to be nervous. I don't bite.

Page.StageDirection: GREG suddenly moves up in her face.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: GREG
DialogueBlock.Line: I'm going to murder you.

Page.StageDirection: He leaves as TONY and PRIYA re-enter.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: So what's the new kid like?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Um. He just said he was going to murder me.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: What?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: What did you do?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: Tony!

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: What? All new kids are a bit jumpy. He might just have
an edgy sense of humour.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: That's not okay whether you're the new kid or not.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: It wasn't even that. He just doesn't seem ... normal.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: I'll say. His aesthetic is on point.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Maybe we just got off on the wrong foot. I don't know.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: That's no excuse for what he did. Tell Miss Duane.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: No, I really dort want to be a bother.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: You're not bothering her by telling her about you
being threatened.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: I don't know. Tyson said that she's not the most reliable
to deal with.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: Of course he said that. He's seen her.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: 'That's a good point. I saw her this morning crying in her car. She was just playing Lana Del Rey on repeat. Shes
really going through it.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: You can still try to talk to her.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: Uh, what about the SRC? They have a meeting with the
principal once a week. And hed trust their word above yours.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: It's worth a shot. Those girls have always liked me enough.
T used to live next door to Madison before she got popular.
Surely we'll still have some stuff in common.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: I mean I guess. You can try.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: I'll help by going to the shops.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: Do you want me to come with?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: All good.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: Text me if you need anything, okay?

Scene.title: The Hall
Scene.description: Stella tries to report Greg's threats to the SRC

DialogueBlock.Speaker: MADISON
DialogueBlock.Line: No, Madyson, the streamers are supposed to be festive!

DialogueBlock.Speaker: MADYSON
DialogueBlock.Line: They are festive.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: MADISON
DialogueBlock.Line: They are clearly ebuliant! I can see that from across the hall, why can't you? Get others!

Page.StageDirection: STELLA enters.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: MADISON
DialogueBlock.Line: Stella, this better be important. The food truck is screwing me on slushie machines and the DJ is double booked. Miss Duane is now running music. She's 23. That's five years too old to be a DJ. What's wrong?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Um, I need to report something to you.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: MADISON
DialogueBlock.Line: I'm flattered you felt comfortable enough to trust me. This is a safe space, okay? No judgement.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: So the new guy.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: MADISON
DialogueBlock.Line: Greg? Cheekbones Greg? I saw him near the science blocks.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Yeah. He's been giving me some trouble.

Page.StageDirection: MADYSON and MADDISON drop everything and swarm in.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: MADYSON
DialogueBlock.Line: Shut the front door.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: MADDISON
DialogueBlock.Line: You have to tell us everything.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: There's not much to tell. He just said he was going to kill me.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: MADDISON
DialogueBlock.Line: Wow. You must have done a number on him.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: MADYSON
DialogueBlock.Line: He's only been here like an hour.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: MADISON
DialogueBlock.Line: What are you two talking about? Stop assuming that just because he's a boy that they're in a relationship. Don't place your lived experiences on someone else, okay? Actually listen. Be the leaders that the school expects us to be.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Thank you Maddie.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: MADISON
DialogueBlock.Line: Madison.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: I really didn't think you were going to help me.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: MADISON
DialogueBlock.Line: Oh babes, I car't help you.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: You're the President of the SRC.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: MADISON
DialogueBlock.Line: Exactly. I look after the whole student body. If word gets out about this, the whole grade could be punished and then there's no dance. My reputation's at stake.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: But what about me? You believe me right?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: MADISON
DialogueBlock.Line: That's not really the point. Honestly, I feel like you're not listening to me.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: MADYSON
DialogueBlock.Line: She isn't.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: MADDISON
DialogueBlock.Line: Seriously, it's disappointing.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: MADISON
DialogueBlock.Line: I've just told you that this is a bigger problem than any one of us and you can't see past yourself. I thought you were better than this.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: What am I supposed to do?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: MADISON
DialogueBlock.Line: You're smart and independent. You'll figure it out. Make sure you make self-care a priority. Check your nails, all that. We have to look out for each other.
Just, you know, quietly. I have to do what's right for everyone. And what's right for everyone is making sure the most important night of their lives goes off.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: The Year 10 Pre-Formal is the most important night.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: MADISON
DialogueBlock.Line: Now youre getting it. We don't talk enough by the way. Let's do this again soon.

Page.StageDirection: She hugs STELLA.

Scene.title: New Kid
Scene.description: A group chat reveals rumors and hype about Greg

Page.StageDirection: Inside a group chat.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: ALL
DialogueBlock.Line: There's a new kid at the school.
I saw him at the science block with Tonksie and Priya and that.
He looks pretty shredded hey.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: MADISON
DialogueBlock.Line: I think it's important we foster a welcoming environment for them.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: MADYSON
DialogueBlock.Line: I liked that.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: MADDISON
DialogueBlock.Line: Yaaaass.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: ALL
DialogueBlock.Line: I heard that he got expelled from his last school for fighting the PE teacher.
Did you guys know that if it gets over 40 degrees they have to legally send us home? It feels like 40 degrees hey?
I sent a gif via Giphy.
He looks like a loose unit hey.
But seriously. Did Tonksie actually lick the toilet or was he faking?
Don't be immature, Trent. Of course he did.
It's a gif from Rupaul's Drag Race.
I heard he hooked up with a teacher and then the teacher changed their name and now they work at a Kmart or something. Dude's a legend.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: MADISON
DialogueBlock.Line: He's got a mysterious thing going on.
I'm not good at using my words.

Page.DialogueBlock: I heard his dad's in prison for breaking his mum out of prison . I heard he burned a pet store down. I heard he used to be Tumblr famous. I heard he applied for Australia's Got Talent and got through to the filmed bit.
Page.StageDirection: Rumours continue to swirl as the voices get louder and each of the kids log off until the last one remains ...

DialogueBlock.Line: I posted a Baby Yoda meme.

Page.StageDirection: Dramatic music.

Scene.title: PE Class
Scene.description: A vicious game of Dodgeball highlighting Greg's aggression

Page.StageDirection: DODGEBALL
The sound of a whistle. MR RIDGE, the PE Teacher has gathered the kids into teams. They're lined up to play. STELLA and GREG are on the same team.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: RIDGE
DialogueBlock.Line: Alright, now. There are some of you that still believe that Phys Ed is a cop out. That it doesn't prepare you for the real world out there. Well, let me just be the one to tell you that there is nothing'that prepares you more for the cruelty and indifference of the world than dodging balls in a game we like to call ... Dodgeball. The game is simple, everyone. Dodge the balls. You're hit, you're out. Work as a team. You ready?

Page.StageDirection: He blows the whistle. Music pumps, it's extreme. The team quickly gets whittled down until STELLA and GREG are the only two left on their team. GREG suddenly throws a ball at STELLA, hard.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: RIDGE
DialogueBlock.Line: Stella. You're out. Let's go mate. Quickly now!

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: But he's on my team!

DialogueBlock.Speaker: RIDGE
DialogueBlock.Line: Don't argue with me madam. You know the rules. You get hit, you're out.
Wouldn't be the first time balls hit her in the face.

Page.StageDirection: Snickers from some of the boys.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: Hey! Six, you have to do something.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: RIDGE
DialogueBlock.Line: Well I'm trying to, Priya, but first we have to make sure this game is fair. Sit down, Stella. You're out.

Page.StageDirection: STELLA sighs and leaves the court. GREG beats the other team effortlessly. We should see a bit more of his vicious streak here.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: RIDGE
DialogueBlock.Line: Oof. You've got an arm, son. Good job. Alright everyone, showers and changed before next period. Stella. A word please.

Page.StageDirection: The other kids leave.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: RIDGE
DialogueBlock.Line: I hope you realise that I don't find it fun to have to stick to the rules all the time. It's nothing personal, but if I've learned one thing in my time as a PE teacher it's that the rules are there for a reason. Do you know why?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: No.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: RIDGE
DialogueBlock.Line: To protect us all. Dodgeball might seem like a silly thing to be strict about, but take this lightly and the rest becomes a joke too, okay? Alright. Good talk. Off you go, love.

Scene.title: Locker Room
Scene.description: Greg confronts Tony and steals Stella's shoes

Page.StageDirection: The boys' locker room. GREG is drinking another of the energy drinks.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: ALL
DialogueBlock.Line: Okay, what about Madyson with a Y?
Would.
So would.
Definitely.
Kim.
Would. She's a solid 8.
What? She's a 7 at best.
What about-you, Tonksie?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: Um, I don't know if giving numbers to the girls is the nicest thing.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: ALL
DialogueBlock.Line: Shut up, Tonksie.
What's the big deal? They're not here.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: What if they gave you numbers?
I wouldn't care. I'd be like a 9.

Page.StageDirection: He burps.

DialogueBlock.Line: At least.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: I just think itd be best if we didn't.

Page.StageDirection: GREG senses weakness.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: GREG
DialogueBlock.Line: You would.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: ALL BOYS
DialogueBlock.Line: Ohbhhhbhhh
Roasted Tonksie!
Greg! Greg! Greg! Greg!
Youd give Priya a number I bet. If you know what I mean.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: ALL BOYS
DialogueBlock.Line: Ooooooooooh.
DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY

DialogueBlock.Line: We're friends.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: GREG
DialogueBlock.Line: Oh, so you wouldn't mind if I had a crack then?

Page.StageDirection: TONY picks up his bag to leave. All other boys are leaving.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: GREG
DialogueBlock.Line: Oh, it's just a joke Tonksie. You're not going to say anything are you?
He's not going to say a thing if he knows what's good for him.

Page.StageDirection: This is a test for TONY. He fails and he knows it.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: I won't.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: GREG
DialogueBlock.Line: Good boy. Now get out.

Page.StageDirection: He does. GREG is alone and starts to change. STELLA enters and sees GREG in octopus form.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Oh, my goodness, I'm sorry!

DialogueBlock.Speaker: GREG
DialogueBlock.Line: GET OUT!

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: But what'd I do?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: GREG
DialogueBlock.Line: Get out of here! NOW!

Page.StageDirection: STELLA doesn't move.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Your legs.
No.
Squish?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: GREG
DialogueBlock.Line: Aren't you a smart arse then. You figured me out. You should have closed the tank when you had the chance.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Oh my goodness ...

DialogueBlock.Speaker: GREG
DialogueBlock.Line: What you gonna do? Nobody will believe you. And then when I smash you, there'll be nobody left to tell on me.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: It is you.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: GREG
DialogueBlock.Line: Yeah brah. I'm here to tear everything down. We all are.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: We?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: GREG
DialogueBlock.Line: Give me your shoes.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: What?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: GREG
DialogueBlock.Line: I like those shoes. Give them to me.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: But I need -

DialogueBlock.Speaker: GREG
DialogueBlock.Line: Now!

Page.StageDirection: STELLA quickly takes off her shoes and hands them to GREG.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: GREG
DialogueBlock.Line: Get out.

Scene.title: Playground
Scene.description: Miss Duane uses "crush logic" to dismiss Greg's bullying

Page.StageDirection: MISS DUANE is on playground duty.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: Stella, where are your shoes?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: ...I can't tell you.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: You will tell me. You're not in full uniform. Tell me what's going on or you'll be on detention for a whole other week.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: But I can't tell you.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: Stella, look, I get it. You don't want to tell on anybody because students think that kids that do that are the weaker, stupider children. And that's kind of true. But if somebody asks you, it's not ratting somebody out. Does that make sense?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Not really —

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: So where are your shoes?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: I went to talk to Greg and he asked to borrow them.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: Borrow them? You sure about that?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Yes. I promise. I don't want there to be any trouble or anything.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: Do you think I was born yesterday? I've been a teacher for almost four months, Stella. This isn't my first time at the rodeo. I know exactly what's going on.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Really? So you can help?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: I sure can. Greg has a crush on you.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: What?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: I'm surprised your mother hasr't had this conversation with you already, you're like what, fifteen? I guess it's up to me then. Have a seat. Have you ever heard of the saying "Treat 'em mean, keep em keen?"

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: No.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: What it means is if a boy is ever mean to you, that's just his way of expressing that he likes you.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: That doesn't seem right.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: It is right. People can't just tell each other they like them. Its a game.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Do you think that this is appropriate?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: To guide the next generation of young people? It's why I got into this job. Treat em mean, Stella. Meeeeeaaaan.

Page.StageDirection: She leaves.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: Are you okay?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: Where are your shoes? Are we not wearing shoes anymore? Is that not cool? Are other kids doing it?

Page.StageDirection: TONY rips off his shoes.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Greg took them.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: Right, that's it, I'm going to Mr Fowler.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: No, it's okay.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: It's so not okay!

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: I need to tell you guys something. Something that's going to be a little weird.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: What is it?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: I think that Squish might be disguising himself as Greg.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: What?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: He smells strange, he only shows up the day after we lose Squish, and just before, I saw him without his shirt he didn't look like a boy should.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: That's crazy though. How did he learn English, or how walk? And how could he have grown that fast?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: Why would he disguise himself as a kid?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: I don't know. But I saw what I saw. He said he was going hurt me and then he made me give him his shoes.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: I'm sorry.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: On the bright side, at least I wont get tinea. They're airing out.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: Neither will I. You can't get it twice.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: Stella, I don't know what you think you saw, but octopuses can't do what you're saying. I agree he's a douche canoe.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: Yeah. Maybe he just seems like a monster because of stuff he's saying. Not an actual monster.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: You guys have to believe me.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: Would you believe me if it was the other way around?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: I'd give you the benefit of the doubt. I've gone to the teachers, and the SRC. I don't know what to do.

Page.StageDirection: TONY and PRIYA look at each other.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: Look, Stella, there is another way.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: But you have to be ready to give up a bit of the high road.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: I just want it to stop.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: Be careful what you wish for.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Just tell me how, okay?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: The Grade 7s.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: What about them?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: Have you ever seen them?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Not really.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: Be grateful you haven't.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: One time I saw them throw a dog onto the roof of the library.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: Nobody crosses the Grade 7s, Stella. They're dangerous enough on their own.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: But they're never on their own. They travel in packs. One minute you're fine, and the next their tiny little hands are tearing you apart.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Why are you telling me all this?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: They'll take care of anyone for a price. You want to beat a monster? You bring a monster.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Okay. Let's do it.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: Tony, get the word to the Grade 7s and we'll arrange a meeting.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: Oh boy.

Scene.title: Behind the Sheds
Scene.description: Tony bargains with the Grade 7 scooter gang

Page.StageDirection: TONY is alone behind the sheds, terrified. There is fast movement in the shadows, eerie giggling. It's very spooky

DialogueBlock.Line: You're a long way from home, Grade 10er. Heeeeheeeheee.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: I'm not here to fight?
It won't be a fight.
Not much of a fight at all.
What do you want, Grade 10er?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: I've come to bargain.
We don't bargain. We demand.

Page.StageDirection: THE GRADE 7s emerge from the shadows in formation on scooters. They possibly have weapons

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: Which one of you is leader?
We don't have a leader.
We move as one.
And you came to the wrong bike racks, kid.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: I can pay
Gift Cards only

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: I know. I brought one.

Page.StageDirection: One of the kids scoots forward and snatches it. She checks it with her phone.

DialogueBlock.Line: Gimme that.
It's a Nandos card. There's like 5 dollars left on it still.
That's what we're worth to you?
You're worth less to us.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: Fine. I have a Typo one as well. Here.
Checks out.
So who's the job?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: A Grade 10er called Greg. He's big, Grade 12 big, but everybody said you can deal with him.
We can deal with whoever we want. Everybody crumbles.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: When will you do it?
Getting jumpy already?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: I'm just not comfortable with this.
Because you're weak. You wouldn't be making us do your dirty work otherwise.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: So when are you doing it?
Soon. Can't say exactly but give us a name and that name gets done. If you tell anyone about this, we'll deny it. And then we'll have a little FaceTime. Snitches get stitches. Got it?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: Yeah.
Good. We're done here. Don't come back.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: Yeahokaythankyou.

Page.StageDirection: TONY runs.

Scene.title: Principal's Office
Scene.description: Mr. Fowler checks in on Greg

Page.StageDirection: MR FOWLER is sitting with GREG.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: FOWLER
DialogueBlock.Line: Greg, firstly, we don't want you to worry, you're not in trouble. I know some of the students freak out a bit when they get called into my office. Freak out. Do kids still say that? Am I saying that right?

Page.StageDirection: Silence.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: FOWLER
DialogueBlock.Line: Matey, this is just a check in to see how youre fitting in with everyone. I know the first week at a new school can be tough. But Miss Duane here is really happy with your performance for in class, and even Mr Ridge says you have a hell of an arm for dodgeball. I haven't seen anyone fit in so well before.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: GREG
DialogueBlock.Line: This school pleases my boy tastes.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: FOWLER
DialogueBlock.Line: That's great to hear, buddy. Any questions for me?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: GREG
DialogueBlock.Line: NO.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: FOWLER
DialogueBlock.Line: Alright then, on your way buddy. My door is always open.

Page.StageDirection: GREG leaves, and is alone. He sniffs the air. The sound of rattling chains. Something is near.

Scene.title: The Ambush
Scene.description: The Grade 7s attack Greg but are brutally defeated

DialogueBlock.Line: We've heard a lot about you, Grade 10er.

Page.StageDirection: THE GRADE 7s emerge on their scooters, armed to the teeth.

DialogueBlock.Line: We've gotta admit it, we kind of admire you.
This isn't personal, you know.
We just really like Nandos.
'There's a price on your head.
And I'm hungry for Peri Peri.
Well make it quick.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: GREG
DialogueBlock.Line: Oh, please don't.
Your funeral, dude.

Page.StageDirection: GREG laughs.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: GREG
DialogueBlock.Line: Finally.

Page.StageDirection: They run at each other as the stage goes dark.

Scene.title: Assembly
Scene.description: Mr. Fowler discusses the "scooter-related" hospitalizations

Page.StageDirection: The bells marks the next day at assembly. The students are all gathered,

DialogueBlock.Speaker: FOWLER
DialogueBlock.Line: I have a good mind to cancel the Pre-Formal entirely! At this school, we have standards of respect and kindness. Of listening to each other. And not resolving our problems with violence! We have half of the Grade 7 cohort in hospital right now! One has a scooter inserted into their bottom!

Page.StageDirection: Laughter from the students.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: We're so dead.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: Shhhhh!

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: How did he get them all? That's like 30 kids.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: FOWLER
DialogueBlock.Line: Oh you think that's funny? That a student had a scooter placed inside their bottom? You think that's funny that I have to explain that to you? They can't sit down! They're in the hospital being suspended from the ceiling with their bare rear end in the air! Where everyone can see it? That's funny to you?

Page.StageDirection: More laughter.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: FOWLER
DialogueBlock.Line: I'll wait.

Page.StageDirection: He does.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: FOWLER
DialogueBlock.Line: The dance will go ahead tonight, and only because the deposits are non-refundable. But mark my words. If there is any trouble, any at all. I will come down on you. Hard. And fast.

Page.StageDirection: More laughter.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: FOWLER
DialogueBlock.Line: Your time, guys, not mine.

Page.StageDirection: MR RIDGE whispers.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: RIDGE
DialogueBlock.Line: Pick up the pace Terry. This is our lunch too.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: FOWLER
DialogueBlock.Line: Good point. On your way now. Dismissed.

Page.StageDirection: The bells sounds again as the students scatter.

Scene.title: The Quad
Scene.description: Greg threatens Tony; Francine rescues Tony on a BMX

Page.StageDirection: SQUISH slowly makes his way to TONY, flanked by some of the boys.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Oh no.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: He knows. We're screwed.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: Go. Go. Get out of here. Quickly.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Just wait a second.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: Read the room, Stella. Be careful, Tony.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: Gol.

Page.StageDirection: of his
STELLA and PRIYA leave. TONY pulls a Redskin out pocket and nervously eats it. GREG appears.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: Oh, hey Greg. How's it hanging?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: GREG
DialogueBlock.Line: What?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: How's it hanging? You know, how are your feelings hanging today? It's a thing I've heard. Pretty cool, hey? I'm kind of the trendsetter around here.
Do you want some of my Redskin? You don't have to have the bit I licked.
Dog act, Tonksie.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: What?
You dog, Tonksie.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: What did I do?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: GREG
DialogueBlock.Line: I should thank you. I haven't had a fight like that in this body yet. It's good to get a feel for it.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: Your body?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: GREG
DialogueBlock.Line: You think the children can protect you?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: What do you mean, your body? Guys, what're you doing siding with him?
He's the loosest unit in Grade 10.
I mean he smashed ail the Grade 7s, Tonksie.
That's hectic.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: GREG
DialogueBlock.Line: Where does the girl live?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: Who?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: GREG
DialogueBlock.Line: You know who.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: I'm not telling.
He licks toilets, you know Greg.
Maybe he'll drink from one. Let's find out.

Page.StageDirection: GREG goes to hit TONY.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: Around the corner from the pet shop! That's all 'm saying.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: GREG
DialogueBlock.Line: Give me your clothes.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: I'm sorry?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: GREG
DialogueBlock.Line: Your clothes. I want them.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: Um, no thank you.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: GREG
DialogueBlock.Line: Give them now or I'll break your teeth.
You better do it Tonksie.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: GREG
DialogueBlock.Line: I'm getting them either way, bruh.

Page.StageDirection: TONY is close to tears.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: GREG
DialogueBlock.Line: Guess were doing it the fun way

Page.StageDirection: As GREG prepares to hit TONY, the lights go wild. There's smoke. A figure flies out from the shadows, jumping on a BMX bike. She is disguised similarly to GREG, although more put together. She skids to a stop between GREG and TONY and throws down a small string of fireworks that explode, blinding GREG.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: FRANCINE
DialogueBlock.Line: Get on!

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: Who are you?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: FRANCINE
DialogueBlock.Line: No time! I'll explain later!

Page.StageDirection: He stands on the back pegs as she pedals away.

Scene.title: The Dance
Scene.description: Greg stalks and threatens Stella during the Cha Cha Slide

Page.StageDirection: It’s going off in the hall as the Formal is in full swing. MISS DUANE is playing the music from her phone. Everyone is dancing is formation. PRIYA is sitting on the side.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: Madison
DialogueBlock.Line: Hiiii everyone, just a reminder that the photobooth only holds eight people, so just take turns or have less friends! Have a good night everyone! You'll remember this night for the rest of your lives.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: I hope not.

Page.StageDirection: STELLA enters.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: Have you seen TOny?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: No, I thought he was with you?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: You don't think...

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: I don't want to.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: I'll go and look for him. Stay here in case he turns up, okay? Text me if you need me.

Page.StageDirection: She leaves.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: No wait! I'll come with you!

Page.StageDirection: She is stopped by MADYSON.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: MADYSON
DialogueBlock.Line: Everybody on the D floor now! That means you!

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: No, I have to go!

Page.StageDirection: MISS DUANE takes the mic

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: Make the most of tonight, everyone, it’s all uphill from here. One day you might DJ a school dance. But for now, here's a song you might remember.

Page.StageDirection: The Cha Cha Slide. STELLA tries to escape, but has no choice but to dance with everybody else. As the song continues, it gets more sinister as GREG appears in the darkness, With each strobe flash, each criss cross, he inches closer and closer to STELLA as she senses with horror that he is near. He ends the song right behind her. Everybody cheers as the song ends, and moves into a slow dance. Everybody pairs up as STELLA pleads for someone, anyone else to dance with. GREG grabs her and pulls her in. For everyone else, this is romantic. For her, it's bone chilling.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Why me?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: GREG
DialogueBlock.Line: I don't know what you mean.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Why did you pick me? Why are you doing this to me?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: GREG
DialogueBlock.Line: It's simple.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Why?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: GREG
DialogueBlock.Line: Because I can. I'm going to take your house and your family and destroy them all. And I'm going to make you watch, Because you're just a nothing human kid and you can do nothing to stop me. You're weak. You told me yourself. You're not special. You have such a long life of misery ahead of you. I'm doing you a favour by tearing it down. And I will. Piece by piece. You asked me if I can do anything special, do you remember? I have many abilities, Stella. There are so many things that I can do. And I will do them all to you. And nobody will help you. Nobody will care, The entire world will let me win.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: You're a monster.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: GREG
DialogueBlock.Line: I know you are, but what am I?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: I won't do it.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: GREG
DialogueBlock.Line: I know you are, but what am I?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: You're not funny.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: GREG
DialogueBlock.Line: I know you are, but what am I?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: You're horrible.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: GREG
DialogueBlock.Line: I know you are, but what am I?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Leave me alone!

Page.StageDirection: She shoves him as the music cuts. Everyone stares as GREG calculates his next move... and runs away. Everyone at the dance looks at STELLA with disgust as the dance melts away.

Scene.title: Stella's Bedroom
Scene.description: Squish/Greg lurks in the shadows; Tyson ignores her

Page.StageDirection: At home, STELLA is lying alone in the dark in her bed. Thunder and lightning. A loud boom of thunder and a flash of lightning shows SQUISH standing at the door. He is closer to his original form, and his teeth are long and sharp, poking out from his 'little boy head'. With each flash of lightning and thunder, he is closer and closer, until his face is right next to STELLAS. She is paralysed by fear. She should be — it's pretty terrifying.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA [whispering]
DialogueBlock.Line: Mum?

Page.StageDirection: SQUISH creeps closer. STELLA dares not look.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Mum?
MUM!

Page.StageDirection: As soon as TYSON enters, SQUISH flattens against the wall, camouflaging himself against the pattern of the wallpaper. He is invisible.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TYSON
DialogueBlock.Line: What's wrong? Mum isn't home yet.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: He's here!

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TYSON
DialogueBlock.Line: Woah. You have a guy over?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: No! Tyson, someone's in my room. Please.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TYSON
DialogueBlock.Line: Mate. You have to stop with the true crime. You're being paranoid.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Just look!

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TYSON
DialogueBlock.Line: Fine.
Nope, nothing. You're probably just dreaming or something.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Can you just stay here for a little bit?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TYSON
DialogueBlock.Line: What? No. I'm... busy. I have a ... friend downstairs.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: You're not listening!

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TYSON
DialogueBlock.Line: And neither should you be. Maybe put headphones on. Goodnight.

Page.StageDirection: He turns off the light and closes the door, leaving the light on. SQUISH leans in close to STELLA.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: SQUISH
DialogueBlock.Line: Goodnight, friend.

Page.StageDirection: Another flash of lightning and he is gone.

Scene.title: Tony's Bedroom
Scene.description: Francine explains Greg is an octopus and they must fight

Page.StageDirection: TONY's room. FRANCINE and TONY enter.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: FRANCINE
DialogueBlock.Line: Close the door. Has he been to your house? Does he know where you live?

Page.StageDirection: TONY is frozen.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: FRANCINE
DialogueBlock.Line: Tony?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: There's a girl in my room.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: FRANCINE
DialogueBlock.Line: Has he been to your house?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: No. Nobody comes to this side of town. And it's just me and my Dad here.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: FRANCINE
DialogueBlock.Line: Where is he?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: Um ... probably out. He works pretty late.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: FRANCINE
DialogueBlock.Line: He can't know that I'm here. Nobody can know I've been here. Do you understand?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: Gotcha.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: FRANCINE
DialogueBlock.Line: Good. I've been looking for you, Tony Tonks. Your friend is in danger.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: Woah. I have goosebumps.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: FRANCINE
DialogueBlock.Line: The one who took your bike. He's not what he seems. You may have felt it. His odd speech patterns. His size. Do you understand?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: I know what you mean.
DialogueBlock.InlineDirection: He got held back a grade.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: FRANCINE
DialogueBlock.Line: He's an octopus.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: gasps.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: Stella was right. Oh man. I feel really awful.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: FRANCINE
DialogueBlock.Line: You can help her, Tony. I'm going to help you. The one who has chosen her has chosen her for a reason, and he won't stop tormenting her until it's too late. He thinks she's weak, and he'll get in her head. He's smart, smarter than a lot of our kind.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: _ FRANCINE

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: Our?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: FRANCINE
DialogueBlock.Line: We're not all here to conquer, Tony. Some of us just want to live here in peace. But we have to stand up for those who can't. Both of us. There's a special power in you. ''m going to show you.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: I [already know.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: FRANCINE
DialogueBlock.Line: What?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: Check this out.

Page.StageDirection: He turns on music and he starts dancing.

Scene.title: FaceTime
Scene.description: Priya tells Stella she can't help

Page.StageDirection: Back in STELLA' room. STELLA is now hiding in her sleeping bag. She FaceTimes PRIYA, who is up playing video games.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: I didn't find Tony. I hope he's okay. My parents took ages to pick me up from the dance.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Priya! You have to help me!

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: I can't hear you.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: I can't speak up. He's here.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: Who's there?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Greg. He's here.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: Oh my God.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: I know. You have to come over.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: I can't.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: What?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: I can't, Stella. ''m sorry. I have too much to lose. If they find out about the Grade 7s and Tony, I won't be able to keep going to uni. I'll be grounded.
