If Greg finds out I was a part of it, it's just ... I don't know. Nothing has worked. Maybe just ignore him. Maybe that's all we can do.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: I can't ignore this.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: I know it's not ideal, but I was thinking about it. My Dad says it's the best thing to do. I mean he still gets trouble from people at work because of his accent and all of that but he ignores them and he's mostly a happy guy. Bad people just exist, Stella. Maybe we just have to let them be bad and try to ignore it.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: I thought you were my friend.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: I'm just being practical. Be safe, okay?

Page.StageDirection: She hangs up.
TONY is still popping and locking. FRANCINE is sitting on the bed watching.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: FRANCINE
DialogueBlock.Line: Tony, we don't have time.

Page.StageDirection: TONY keeps dancing. FRANCINE turns off the music.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: My tunes!

DialogueBlock.Speaker: FRANCINE
DialogueBlock.Line: Tony, we have to focus. How long has he been here?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: Since Monday.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: FRANCINE
DialogueBlock.Line: And his speech has improved?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: A little.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: FRANCINE
DialogueBlock.Line: He'll keep learning, Tony. All octopuses learn from their surroundings. If he sees it, he'll copy it. If he hears it, he'll use it. We adapt. That’s how we survive. Have you told him anything?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: Ummmm... he asked about Stella.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: FRANCINE
DialogueBlock.Line: And what did you tell him?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: Where she lived.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: FRANCINE
DialogueBlock.Line: What?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: I'm sorry.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: FRANCINE
DialogueBlock.Line: He'll learn everything about her now! She was in danger before, but now he could be too strong!

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: I'm sorry!

Page.StageDirection: TONY flinches as if he expects to be hit.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: FRANCINE
DialogueBlock.Line: I'm not going to hurt you.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: I'm sorry! I messed it up! I'm sorry! I know I screw everything up!

DialogueBlock.Speaker: FRANCINE
DialogueBlock.Line: Tony, we have to work on a plan.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: Everyone always says I’m just an idiot.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: FRANCINE
DialogueBlock.Line: Who says that?
Tony?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: I don't think I can help you. I'll just stay here. I like it here. It's pretty nice, and I get to do whatever I want. I make my own dinner, and I can eat whatever I want, and I can play my music as loud as I want as long as Dad isn't home, and if he is, I can just stay in here and I write stories. I can feel pretty important here. But I know I'm not. It's fine.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: FRANCINE
DialogueBlock.Line: Where I come from, the weak are left to fend for themselves. When I was born, my mother and father found it difficult to find food for themselves, let alone all of us. My mother wanted to eat me because I was one of the weakest of all of us. She thought it was what was best for the whole clutch. But my father decided not too. They decided that I was better off on my own. So, they left me. And I struggled, and struggled, and endured, and survived. I hid, and concealed myself, and survived. And you will too. You are too important not to.

It's a painful life, being an outsider. You will see things differently. You will be ridiculed for it. You'll be shunned for it. People will think you are not worth their time. But above all you will see beautiful things, because you are different. I've never travelled in my clutch, so nobody ever noticed me. I saw the most glorious corners of the ocean because of it. I've been to places and learned things that only dream of. And I am here now. And I did that all on my own. But you won't have to. You have your own tribe.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: How did you know about them?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: FRANCINE
DialogueBlock.Line: I've been watching you at school.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: Then why haven't you come sooner?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: FRANCINE
DialogueBlock.Line: I'm in Grade 12. Grade 12's never talk to Grade 10's.

There is a fight coming, Tony Tonks. You have an important part to play in it. You will be there for your friend, and I will teach you all that you need to know. About your enemy. About your gift. And you will rise above all of this. I promise you.
Let's begin.

Scene.title: The Classroom
Scene.description: A failed "Talking Totem" intervention

Page.StageDirection: STELLA appears in the classroom, where MISS DUANE and MR FOWLER are waiting.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: You wanted to see me?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: Stella, have a seat please.
What I am about to say, I want you to understand that I am saying it with no judgement, okay?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Yeah.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: Good. Now, it has been brought to my attention that there are some people in your life that are a little worried about you.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: They are?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: Super worried. It has come to my attention that you are smack bang in the middle of a bully problem. And I want to say that I am so sorry I didn't pick up the signs earlier, Stella. I'm sorry for letting you down.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: FOWLER
DialogueBlock.Line: We have a zero tolerance policy with bullying here at the school, Stella. This is serious.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Oh thank you! I've been trying to tell everyone!

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: Why would you tell everyone that you're a bully?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: I'm not —

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: It's time to get everything out in the open. Sit down. I'm bring the others in.

Page.StageDirection: Come in please, everyone.
The class enters, followed by a sheepish GREG. GREG is sniffling like he's been crying. MISS DUANE produces a small totem.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: Alright, let's sit in a half circle. It hurts my heart to see such good friends involved in this, and it's a shame that Tony is off sick so he can't be a part of this, but won't it be nice for him to come back to all of his friends getting along?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: I don't understand.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: Well, someone needs to keep up! No judgement.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: FOWLER
DialogueBlock.Line: No judgement.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: Now, this is the talking totem. Whoever has this is the only one who can talk, and the rest of us listen. This is a safe space. There are no wrong things to say, but we need to respect the feelings of everyone else. Now, Greg, would you like to start? We want you to begin whatever you say with “I feel.”

Page.StageDirection: She hands the totem to GREG. As this progresses, the totem is passed to whoever talks.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: GREG
DialogueBlock.Line: I feel really lonely, and I feel sad, and all I wanted to do was be your friends, and you're not letting me be your friends, and it's really hard starting at a new school because I don't know anyone and everyone is meeeeeeeaaaaaannnnn to meeeee!

Page.StageDirection: He cries.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: Thank you, Greg. That was really, really brave, buddy.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: MADISON
DialogueBlock.Line: The other day you tried to abuse my power to benefit your own interests. I just honestly feel like you've lost perspective of your own selfishness. I'm not perfect and I own that, but this is honestly such an opportunity for growth.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: MADDISON
DialogueBlock.Line: You're so right.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: MADYSON
DialogueBlock.Line: Yeah.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: Does anybody else have anything they'd like to share? Greg's pretty cool once you get to know him. Like, you just have to lighten up Stella. He roasts everyone, you don't need to be so defensive. Why can't anybody take a joke anymore? STELLA is being worn down. We're supposed to treat strangers with kindness Stella. I used to look up to you honestly.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: FOWLER
DialogueBlock.Line: This is a school that is founded on respect and trust, Stella. Lead by example.

Page.StageDirection: PRIYA takes the talking totem.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: Just ... remember what we talked about.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: You know all of this is wrong.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: Please. Just let it go. For all of us.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: Thank you Priya. Stella, do you see how people might feel victimised by you and your behaviour?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: I haven't done anything!

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: It doesn't matter anymore! Just try and listen to your friends!

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: You can't even listen to me!

DialogueBlock.Speaker: GREG
DialogueBlock.Line: Please stop fighting, I just want all of us to be friends again!

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: Let's turn it down, we're upsetting Greg.

Page.StageDirection: STELLA jumps up and screams. She's had enough.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Upsetting Greg?! You're all worried about Greg?! Ever since he got here, he has been nothing but be horrible to me, and nobody cares! He has said to me he wants to kill me, and hurt me, and make me feel like I'm nothing, and I have never, ever done anything to deserve it! You asked me to help him, but before I could even try, he said he was going to kill me. Before I said anything. And I feel like he will. I'm scared and I have told you so many times that I'm scared! The second my best friend thinks I've done something wrong, she leaves me. I feel like nothing. You have made me nothing! And why would anybody listen to nothing? Why won't you listen?!

Page.StageDirection: Long beat.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: Someone wasn't holding the Talking Totem. I'm going to give it to you, Stella, but I won't have any more tantrums, okay? Now, what would you like to say to Greg?

Page.StageDirection: STELLA is broken. The other kids are unsettled for the first time.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: I'm sorry.

Page.StageDirection: MISS DUANE applauds.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: Oh my God, that's the first time that's worked! Yaaaaaay! You're friends again. Now, I need to be on hat duty. You guys go and hang out, okay?

Page.StageDirection: DUANE and PRIYA exit. A gravelly laugh from GREG.

Scene.title: The Quad
Scene.description: Greg challenges Stella to a fight at the flagpole

DialogueBlock.Speaker: GREG
DialogueBlock.Line: Dirt, or water?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Leave me alone.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: GREG
DialogueBlock.Line: Answer the question. Dirt or water?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: I don't even know what you mean.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: GREG
DialogueBlock.Line: It's funny what happens to a human body in the water. It sustains the creatures of the ocean. The fish will feed on your eyes, your tongue, your skin. Even your bones will be given to the ocean. And there's nothing you can do.

Page.StageDirection: STELLA takes a deep breath. With no confidence:

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: I'll fight you.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: GREG
DialogueBlock.Line: What?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: If I have to fight you, that's what I'll do.

Page.StageDirection: GREG advances on her.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: GREG
DialogueBlock.Line: Good.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Tomorrow. 3 o'clock, at the flagpole. That's where we'll fight. The whole school will be able to see then.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: GREG
DialogueBlock.Line: You better show up. If you don't, I'll find you wherever you are and we'll fight anyway. And trust me — you don't want that to happen. What's that on your shirt?

Page.StageDirection: STELLA looks down and GREG punches her, hard in the stomach. She is winded. GREG is laughing. All of the kids see this and do nothing.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: GREG
DialogueBlock.Line: I learned that one today. It's hilarious. I did it to a dog this morning. He had no idea what was on his shirt! It was awesome. What now, pussy? You going to cry? You going to have a little cry? Humans are so gross, dude.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: GREG
DialogueBlock.Line: Enjoy your last night on earth. Tomorrow, 3 o'clock, flagpole. That's where you die.

Page.StageDirection: He leaves. STELLA starts sobbing. MISS DUANE enters.

Scene.title: Stella's House
Scene.description: Tyson teaches Stella how to "throw hands"

Page.StageDirection: STELLA is crying on the ground, alone.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: Stella? What's wrong?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: He's going to kill me.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: I'm disappointed. I thought we had this settled.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: I'm not lying, he hit me, and he said he was going to kill me!

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: He said that? Just now?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Yeah.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: But he probably won't.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: He said it over and over and over.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: I say that I'm going to kill my stepfather all the time, but he's still alive. People say things when they're angry and they just turn out to be empty threats. Heck, people say they're going to do things all the time, but they never do them. It just makes them feel important, like giving blood or drinking almond milk. You and Greg will be back to being friends in no-time.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: We were never friends!

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: Well that can't be true. I asked you to be his friend.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: You're not listening to me!

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: On the contrary. I'm listening more than you'll ever know. No more tears. I'll see you tomorrow. [To herself] You're doing it, Ellie. You're reaching them.

Page.StageDirection: She leaves.
STELLA is crying on the ground, alone.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TYSON
DialogueBlock.Line: Oi gronk. Mum gave me her card, What do you want for dinner?

Page.StageDirection: STELLA doesn't respond.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TYSON
DialogueBlock.Line: Alright. We're having tacos.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: [into the floor] I'm not hungry.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TYSON
DialogueBlock.Line: What's gotten into you?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Nothing.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TYSON
DialogueBlock.Line: Um, okay. You're a terrible liar.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: It's Greg.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TYSON
DialogueBlock.Line: Who's Greg?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: This ... guy at school.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TYSON
DialogueBlock.Line: And what's up with this guy?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: I have to fight him.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TYSON
DialogueBlock.Line: What? Why?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Because he'll fight me. I don't have a chance.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TYSON
DialogueBlock.Line: Probably not. So why are you fighting him?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Because I don't have a choice.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TYSON
DialogueBlock.Line: Alright then.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: So you're not going to say 'you always have a choice'?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TYSON
DialogueBlock.Line: Sometimes you don't. I might not be an amazing brother but I'm not going to lie to you.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: He's got in everyone's head and turned them against me. I'm so screwed.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TYSON
DialogueBlock.Line: Oh, he's that type, is he?

Page.StageDirection: Beat.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TYSON
DialogueBlock.Line: Alright, stand up.
Feet apart. Show me how you move.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: What?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TYSON
DialogueBlock.Line: How do you move? Don't stand still, you're dead if you do. I'm coming for you.

Page.StageDirection: He starts trying to spar with STELLA.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: You don't know how to fight.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TYSON
DialogueBlock.Line: In Grade 8 Daniel Strickland saw me with a guy I liked from St. Augustine's and he tried to start me. I figured out that I needed to learn to throw hands.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Oh, Ty I'm sorry.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TYSON
DialogueBlock.Line: So was Daniel. Keep moving.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Why are you helping me? You never

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TYSON
DialogueBlock.Line: Don't cross your feet. Side step. Don't let them cross. I can push you over real easy if you do, see?

Page.StageDirection: He does.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Ow! Don't trip me!

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TYSON
DialogueBlock.Line: I didn't. You did.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Ty, stop!

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TYSON
DialogueBlock.Line: Oh, is that how you stop a fight? Just say stop? Come on. Side step.

Page.StageDirection: She does, awkwardly.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: I feel stupid.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TYSON
DialogueBlock.Line: You look stupid. But it's the right way to learn. Alright, you want to fight dirty okay? He's bigger than you, there's no shame in it. Eyes, these bits ... His groin. Whatever you have to do. Does he have bits to kick?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: I don't know.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TYSON
DialogueBlock.Line: We'll google that.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: But —

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TYSON
DialogueBlock.Line: Mm?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: I just — if I fight him, I'm no better than he is.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TYSON
DialogueBlock.Line: He doesn't care about being better! Toxic things like him never do. It's just another thing they want to hold over you simply because you care about something, and they care about nothing apart from playing you against yourself. That's what makes them toxic. They just exist to be the opposite of you and I don't know about you but the world doesn't need anyone else not caring. Look at me.
Stella.

Page.StageDirection: Their eyes meet.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TYSON
DialogueBlock.Line: You can take the high road. But that only works if the other guy takes a road to start with. He doesn't care about changing. He doesn't care about how you feel and he doesn't want to listen. He's not capable of it. Everything he does in bad faith, and he knows that rules are your weakness.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Like Batman?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TYSON
DialogueBlock.Line: Oh Batman? Do you know how messed up it is that Batman lets so much bad stuff happen because of his own superiority complex? He's a rich guy who should be in therapy and getting more sunshine. Don't be Batman. You have an opportunity here.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: How do you know all of this about him?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TYSON
DialogueBlock.Line: Babe, I've spent five minutes on the internet. There is nothing special about this thing you're fighting and I don't know whether to laugh or cry about that.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Or do you want me to fight him?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TYSON
DialogueBlock.Line: No.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TYSON
DialogueBlock.Line: I didn't think so. Look, I'm here if you need me. I'll be there tomorrow. But I also know this is your fight. This is your time to say that enough is enough, Stella. I know you can say it loud enough.

Page.StageDirection: He places a hand on her shoulder.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TYSON
DialogueBlock.Line: You can do this. Footwork is half the battle. The battle is the other half.

Page.StageDirection: He leaves. PRIYA and TONY dive through the window.

Scene.title: Resistance
Scene.description: Priya and Tony return to help Stella research octopus weaknesses

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: What are you doing here? Tony! You're okay!

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: My stomach hurts but I think that's the sugar. I'm starting to think it's not the best for you.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: Tony came and got me. He told me everything.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: You found out?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: You were right.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: He's an octopus.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: And a dick. I'm sorry I was a chode. I was wrong.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: I know that was hard for you Priya.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: Thanks. Seriously though. I was a chode. We're running out of time. I think Greg made a shank in art class today.

Page.StageDirection: SQUISH is making a paper mache knife in Art. MISS DUANE is walking past, on her phone.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: Greg, that doesn't look like a dreamcatcher to me. What have you got there?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: GREG
DialogueBlock.Line: Christmas tree.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: Well, look, it's not what I asked you to make, so I can't give you a gold star. This will have to do.

Page.StageDirection: She puts a happy rainbow sticker on his fake head.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: Now put it away or it'll be mine. [off] Jordan. Head out of the kiln now buddy. I won't say it again.

Page.StageDirection: Back to the kids.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Thanks you guys. I just wish it wasn't me.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: So do we. But you know what? It is. And there's nothing you can do about it but fight back. You're in control here. Look at me.

Page.StageDirection: Their eyes meet.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: You're going to fight that butthole tomorrow. And we're going to help you.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: We're not going to let our bud get smacked around by an octopus.

Page.StageDirection: PRIYA sets up her easel and drawing pad. She flips through her research as she continues. Old oil paintings of whaling ships being attacked by giant squids. A photo of an octopus eating a bird. Giant Krakens, squids, Cthulhu, the octopus that predicted the World Cup.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: I've gone to the library and did some research —

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: Nerd.
Sorry. Habit.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: — And everything that has been happening actually makes sense. This isn't the first time that somebody's been victimised by octopuses. There's a really long history of it. Ships being overturned by sea monsters. Whales disappearing. Entire cults devoted to them. They can fit through cracks. They camouflage. They use tools. What do you think that means?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: I don't know.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: I think it's been building up to something. Something big. Tony?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: We did some research —

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: We?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: Me and a friend.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: We're your friends.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: I have a life outside of you guys.

Page.StageDirection: We found this on YouTube. Some Grade Five kid in Russia took it, it was a fight at his school. The bigger kid kind of looks like Greg.
He shows STELLA the video. We hear kids yelling in Russian, scuffling.
We thought it was just another fight, but then ...

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: He ate him!

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: They're getting bolder. There's more videos just like that one from all over the world. It's not just you, Stella. It's kids like you and it's happening everywhere and nobody's standing up to them. You have to stop him.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: But why me?!

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: You're still on this? It doesn't matter why. It is you, yeah, but if it wasn't you, it would be somebody else.

Page.StageDirection: As PRIYA continues, a horrifying vision of multiple GREGS appear all around them in the darkness.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: And if you were in his position, you'd bully other kids too. Think about it. If you were bigger and stronger, of course you'd pick on somebody who wouldn't fight back. People have been doing that ever since there have been people. And he won't stop with you. He'll keep doing it until every octopus in the world isn't scared anymore, and then our mums and dads and brothers and sisters will all be in trouble. We're not going to let that happen.

Page.StageDirection: TONY puts his hand to his ear and makes a 'ring ring' sound.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Tony, what are you doing?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: Just a second. Hello? [answering his fake phone] it's Destiny calling. [listens and then hands her his imaginary phone] It's for you.

Page.StageDirection: STELLA sighs.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: I don't know how I can win on my own.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: Have you even been listening? You're not on your own.

Page.StageDirection: PRIYA

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: I've got an idea to help you, it'll take a lot of luck and every bit of my STEM skills but I think it's the best plan we have.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: And I brought Sour Patch Kids.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: We'll need them. It's going to be a long night.

Scene.title: Training Montage
Scene.description: The trio builds armor and prepares for battle

Page.StageDirection: Power Montage Music. PRIYA' plan is displayed. There's mechanical drawings and blueprints. Treadmills. TONY has a stack of books on marine life, and is reading The Old Man and the Sea.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: Anything we learn about them helps, Tony. What have you got?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: The fish is a metaphor.

Page.StageDirection: More training/ building. Some spot welding. The music stops and TYSON enters.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TYSON
DialogueBlock.Line: We didn't make a decision earlier. Do you want tacos?
What's going on in here?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY/PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: Oh hey Tyson what's up

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TYSON
DialogueBlock.Line: You guys are weird.

Page.StageDirection: He closes the door and training continues.
Elsewhere, SQUISH is bench pressing a terrified preschooler. He drops them, drinks more energy drink and roars.
PRIYA is working on the armour and attaching the pieces to STELLA. TONY is eating Killer Pythons and quizzing her.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: Which breeds of octopus are poisonous?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: All of them!

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: Primary movement mechanism?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Jet propulsion and tentacle manipulation!

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: How many hearts do they have?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Three!

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: How many bones?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: None!

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: How many souls?

Page.StageDirection: STELLA slams down her helmet.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: NONE.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: Ooch. I just got tinglies.

Scene.title: The Flagpole
Scene.description: The final battle between Stella and Squish

Page.StageDirection: FRIDAY, 2:55PM The flagpole. SQUISH is punching a brick wall. All of the kids are gathered. Suddenly, the lights dim. Everything is highly stylised from this point. Smoke, flashing lights. It is STELLA. She is fitted in armour fashioned from random sports goods, kitchen wear, and other household items, but it looks rad. She has a small bumbag across her body.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: SQUISH
DialogueBlock.Line: Ready to die, brah?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: We know your plans, Squish. We know there are others like you.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: SQUISH
DialogueBlock.Line: Doesn't matter. We're smarter than you, we're stronger than you. And I'm going to show you right now why.

Page.StageDirection: He pulls two long, neon pink whips out of his backpack. He begins swinging them around.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: What are they?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: SQUISH
DialogueBlock.Line: Jellyfish stingers. I ripped them straight off this pussy swimming by this morning just for you.

Page.StageDirection: OCTOPUSES ACTUALLY DO THIS. IT'S MESSED UP. Ready?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Ready.

Page.StageDirection: The school bell rings. It's three o'clock.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: ALL
DialogueBlock.Line: FILIMIGGGGGHHHHHTTTT!

Page.StageDirection: They run straight at each other. SQUISH's whips wrap around STELLA' armour, tangling her. SQUISH begins hauling her toward him, but as he is in reach, she headbutts him. He lets go, and she frees herself. It's a vicious scuffle, trading punches and kicks. It's like a Kaiju film — lots of grappling between two titans. STELLA lands a solid punch on SQUISH 's face, and sends him back.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: Priya, I don't feel so good.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: I told you not to eat sugar!

Page.StageDirection: SQUISH retreats to his backpack and grabs a water bomb filled with a dark liquid.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: SQUISH
DialogueBlock.Line: Get some ink up ya!

Page.StageDirection: SQUISH throws the balloon. Ink splatters across STELLA' helmet, blinding her. SQUISH jumps on her back and latches on. She tries to shake him off, but his suckers have attached to her. STELLA runs back into the pole. It bends, SQUISH falls off, STELLA rips off her helmet. She can see again. She turns around and SQUISH lands a punch straight to the stomach, winding her. It looks bad. PRIYA is horrified, TONY is eating a Killer Python and shivering.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: No! Get up, Stella! You have to get up! Tony, we have to do something!

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: Um ... I don't know —

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: Think!

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: So... much ... sugar.

Page.StageDirection: SQUISH readies the knockout punch. TONY takes another bite of the Killer Python. He shakes violently. FRANCINE suddenly appears on her bike.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: FRANCINE
DialogueBlock.Line: Now, Tony! Do it! Just like we talked about!

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: I don't know if I can.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: FRANCINE
DialogueBlock.Line: You have to! I believe in you! Do it!

Page.StageDirection: TONY eats the last part of the Killer Python. He seizures. -PRIYA Tony!

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: That was my name once. Now I am eternal.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: What?

Page.StageDirection: TONY shakes more violently as a booming sound rumbles in a crescendo to silence. Everything is beautiful, time slows almost to a stop around TONY. There is peaceful music. He gently brushes the hair out of STELLAS face. He wipes the ink off STELLA' helmet and places it back on her head.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: FRANCINE
DialogueBlock.Line: You're almost there, Tony.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: To where?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: FRANCINE
DialogueBlock.Line: To your part to play.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: I thought this was it.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: FRANCINE
DialogueBlock.Line: She needs to know what she's fighting for. You need to help her.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: What?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: FRANCINE
DialogueBlock.Line: You have to help her.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: You want me to take her place.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: FRANCINE
DialogueBlock.Line: I'm so sorry, Tony.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: I thought this was my chance to be something else.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: FRANCINE
DialogueBlock.Line: You will be. But you need to do this, Tony. I promise things will change, but you have to do this this one time. And they can't know how.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: Nobody knows anyway.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: FRANCINE
DialogueBlock.Line: Are you ready?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: It's just like every other day. And nobody knows.

Page.StageDirection: Everything moves back to normal. SQUISH swings at TONY, landing a thunderous blow to his stomach.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: SQUISH
DialogueBlock.Line: What?!

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: What just happened? Tony!

Page.StageDirection: SQUISH laughs and takes out his phone to take photos.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: SQUISH
DialogueBlock.Line: You feeling strong, my man? You think you can all just swim around in the ocean like you own it?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: But I don't swim —

DialogueBlock.Speaker: SQUISH
DialogueBlock.Line: You humans want to behave like we do? Maybe I'll be a human boy for a bit.
You're all so gross. You heard of tea bagging bruh? It's all the rage in private schools.

Page.StageDirection: SQUISH stands over TONY's face with his phone, ready to humiliate him. STELLA is struggling to get up.
This'll be awesome when it's mailed to the TikTok or whatever you do.
STELLA is furious, She ties her hair up and back.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Hey!

DialogueBlock.Speaker: SQUISH
DialogueBlock.Line: You're dead.

Page.StageDirection: She spits blood on the floor. With a gutteral venom

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: I know you are. But what am I?

Page.StageDirection: SQUISH throws his phone down and takes a swing, he misses. STELLA unleashes hell on him, landing punch after punch. They continue to fight as the action crosses to THREE KIDS with a large scale model of a city.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: KID 1
DialogueBlock.Line: I now call to order the first Junior City Planners' meeting for the school year. And, might I add, no drop in members three years running.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: KID 2
DialogueBlock.Line: Eat it, Junior Accountants!

DialogueBlock.Speaker: KID 3
DialogueBlock.Line: And what better way to celebrate than a full scale model of Tokyo district, complete with the iconic Shibuya Crossing!

Page.StageDirection: It lights up.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: ALL
DialogueBlock.Line: Building a better tomorrow — today!

Page.StageDirection: STELLA and SQUISH suddenly crash through the model as they continue scuffling. The KIDS scatter, screaming. More fighting as the rest of the kids join the fight. THE GRADE 7s appear, and attack. STELLA manages to rip off SQUISH® disguise, revealing a gross, giant octopus head. He looks terrifying. He roars.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: SQUISH
DialogueBlock.Line: Why won't you stay down?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: I'll never stay down!

Page.StageDirection: STELLA reaches into the bum bag and pulls out firecrackers. She throws them on the ground, and they pop and flash. SQUISH screams. STELLA lands a punch square on SQUISHS face. Somehow in all of this, the entire cohort gets their hits in, including THE GRADE 7s. They overwhelm SQUISH as he is weakened.

STELLA and SQUISH are at a standstill, ready to finish this. SQUISH tries to punch her, but STELLA goes low and grabs him around the waist. She lifts with all of her might. SQUISH goes high in the air, and STELLA holds him for a frozen moment.
PRIYA is stunned. TONY is in a sugar coma. MISS DUANE is vaping at the school fence on the other side of the block, on her phone.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: Jordan, hat on buddy. Won't say it again.

Page.StageDirection: Back to our action as STELLA slams SQUISH down to the ground. Flashing lights and stuff. SQUISH is flattened on the floor. STELLA is lying face down. She's not moving.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: Oooooh we are in so much trouble.

Page.StageDirection: STELLA slowly picks herself up. The kids run over to her, shouting over the top of each other about how awesome the fight was. TONY catches a glimpse of FRANCINE, and goes over to her.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: Did you see?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: FRANCINE
DialogueBlock.Line: I saw. You did it, Tony. You helped your friend. Tony, I meant what I said. Your life today, as it is. It won't always be this way. You have to believe me.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: How do you know?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: FRANCINE
DialogueBlock.Line: I don't know how. But I know you. And I know that you'll be fine. I hope we cross paths again someday.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: You're leaving?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: FRANCINE
DialogueBlock.Line: I am. There are others like him, and so there are other children to warn. My role isn't played out yet. But I'm afraid our time together has.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: But I don't even know your name.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: FRANCINE
DialogueBlock.Line: Well, you can't really pronounce it without a beak between your legs. You can just call me Francine.
You're so much bigger than the world you are living in, Tony Tonks. Please remember that. I believe in you.

Page.StageDirection: She kisses him on the cheek and rides away on her bike.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: TONY
DialogueBlock.Line: How can we ever repay you?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: FRANCINE
DialogueBlock.Line: Stop throwing things in the ocean!

Page.StageDirection: She is gone. MISS DUANE enters.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: WHAT IS GOING ON?! Greg, you need to get up right now.

Page.StageDirection: SQUISH doesn't move. She pokes him with her foot.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: Greg?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: Um ... I think Greg is gone, miss.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: Priya, please. I'm disciplining.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: Look at his face.

Page.StageDirection: She pushes his body with her foot, looking at his true face.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: Oh god, it's like a bag of wet bread rolls. What is it?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: PRIYA
DialogueBlock.Line: He wasn't who he said he was.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: DUANE
DialogueBlock.Line: Ugh. He'd get along great with my ex. Well whatever he was, nobody else needs to know. None of us were here. I'm not losing my job over another body.

Page.StageDirection: Stella Bunt, I am so disappointed in you. I'd expect violent outbursts from her and pretty much anything from him, but from you? What do you have to say for yourself?

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STELLA
DialogueBlock.Line: Grade 10 is a big year.

Page.StageDirection: The STORYTELLERS from the prologue enter once more.

Scene.title: Epilogue
Scene.description: The Storytellers recount the aftermath and Stella's victory

Page.StageDirection: The STORYTELLERS from the prologue enter once more.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STORYTELLERS
DialogueBlock.Line: The victory at the flagpole marked the turning point in the Grade Ten resistance.
The children shared what they had learned with Grade Tens all over the world and slowly, news came in of fights being won everywhere.
Things had changed. Kids weren't afraid of stepping out into the playground anymore.
They felt better about themselves, they had hope. They felt safe.
Priya went back to her one class a week at uni, changing classes from STEM to marine biology.
You can't ever be too careful.

DialogueBlock.Speaker: STORYTELLERS
DialogueBlock.Line: Tony will tell you all about his supposed time warp and tried to reach that peak again, but had to abandon his quest after temporary sugar blindness.
He had to take two weeks off sick after getting chickenpox from Gary Gleeson.
He licked Gary's face.
He made six dollars for doing it.
And Stella?
She changed after that day. She stood a little taller, walked around with bigger steps.
She helps other kids who felt like she did. She was right all along.
It was a special year for not just her, but for everyone she gave hope to.
Things may still be tough in the outside world, but she made the playground safer again.
She taught us all the best lesson.
That people — or octopuses — can only make you feel small if you let them.
And after that day, none of us ever did.

Page.StageDirection: STELLA gets up, puts on her backpack, and strides home. The End.
